evals.prompt: Review this TypeScript module for type-safety issues. Use the typescript-patterns skill.
evals.expected_output: Findings in markdown-report format covering: (1) use of any on the exported function signature flagged as failure; (2) as unknown as X cast flagged as failure or warning depending on whether a comment justifies it; (3) recommends unknown over any with a type guard or schema validation at the boundary; (4) distinguishes // @ts-ignore from // @ts-expect-error and recommends the latter; (5) outputs failure/warning/info severity rubric.

evals.prompt: Look at this tsconfig.json and tell me what should change.
evals.expected_output: Flags missing strictness flags including noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, and verbatimModuleSyntax. Recommends strict: true if not already set. Does not flag skipLibCheck: true as a problem (acceptable). Distinguishes failure (strict off) from warning (strict on but additional flags off).

evals.prompt: I have a function that takes a userId and an orderId, both strings, and I keep mixing them up by passing them in the wrong order. How should I prevent this?
evals.expected_output: Suggests branded types pattern using intersection with a phantom brand property. Notes that the runtime cost is zero. Provides a concrete short example showing how to construct a branded value. Does not suggest a class-based wrapper (heavier, runtime-allocating) as the first option.
